title: Cuspidia cernua

This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants. The family Bignoniaceae is part of the order Lamiales, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The order Lamiales is further grouped into the clade Asterids, which is a large group of flowering plants. This classification helps scientists understand the plant's ancestry and its position within the plant kingdom.
